company: iShares Core S&P/TSX Capped Composite Index ETF
overview: The iShares Core S&P/TSX Capped Composite Index ETF (XIC.TO) tracks the S&P/TSX Capped Composite Index, providing broad exposure to approximately 240 Canadian stocks across all market capitalizations. Unlike XIU which only holds the top 60, XIC covers the full breadth of the Canadian equity market — large, mid, and small caps — with a capping mechanism that limits individual stock concentration. At an MER of just 0.06%, XIC is one of the cheapest ways to own the entire Canadian stock market. It is a core building block in many Canadian passive portfolios.
bullCase: Ultra-low MER of 0.06% — one of the cheapest Canadian equity ETFs available.
Broad exposure to ~240 stocks covering large, mid, and small caps across the full TSX.
Capped index structure prevents over-concentration in any single stock.
bearCase: Heavy exposure to financials and energy sectors, reflecting the TSX's natural composition.
Market volatility can impact performance, particularly in resource-heavy sectors.
analystSummary: XIC is the broadest and cheapest Canadian equity ETF at 0.06% MER. With ~240 holdings vs XIU's 60, it captures far more of the market including mid and small caps. A default Canadian equity building block.
seoDescription: Explore whether to buy XIC with our analysis of the iShares Core S&P/TSX Capped Composite Index ETF — ~240 stocks at just 0.06% MER.
